description: Dedicated git_status (parsed file lists by state) and git_diff (per-file hunks with metadata) tools with structured JSON output.
title: Structured git_status and git_diff tools

description: relay_ping and relay_info exist but return raw data. Add relay_score tool that combines latency, NIP support, uptime history into a normalized score. Could inform automatic relay selection in fetch/publish.
title: Relay health scoring and ranking

description: No templating for common event patterns. Add template registry for common patterns (reply-to-note, quote-repost, reaction, tagged-note) that the agent can invoke with minimal params instead of manually constructing tags.
title: Event composition templates

description: nostr_fetch appends all events from SubscribeMany without dedup. When querying multiple relays, duplicate events (same event ID) are returned. Add seen-map dedup like relay_memory.go already does.
title: nostr_fetch: deduplicate events from multi-relay SubscribeMany

description: Each nostr_publish signs+publishes one event. Add nostr_publish_batch that accepts an array of event specs, signs all, publishes in parallel. Reduces round-trips for multi-event workflows like list updates + note + reaction.
title: nostr_publish_batch: sign and publish multiple events

description: Process handles, structured bash output, git tools, and test runner for agent self-iteration workflows
title: REPL Powers: Agent Development Loop Tools

close_reason: Created history_sanitize.go with SanitizeConversationHistory: drops orphan tool results, drops empty messages, collapses consecutive same-role text, synthesizes error results for trailing unmatched tool calls. Wired into buildLLMMessagesFromTurn with ToolCallRef→ToolCall conversion. 13 tests pass.
description:
New internal/agent/history_sanitize.go with SanitizeConversationHistory(). Rules: drop orphan tool results (no matching assistant ToolCalls), synthesize error results for trailing unmatched tool calls, collapse consecutive same-role plain text, drop empty non-structural messages. Apply in buildLLMMessagesFromTurn before provider call.

Files: internal/agent/history_sanitize.go (new), internal/agent/llm.go
Tests: internal/agent/history_sanitize_test.go (new)
title: Add conversation history sanitization before LLM calls

description: DVM handler exists for receiving jobs but agent has no tool to submit kind:5xxx job requests to other DVMs and poll for kind:6xxx/7000 results. Add nostr_dvm_request tool with optional polling.
title: nostr_dvm_request: submit DVM jobs and poll results

description: Agent-reported stiff joints and science projects. Covers profile updates, dedup, DVM polling, outbox routing, batch signing, structured errors, WoT caching, relay scoring.
title: Agent tool surface improvements

close_reason: RunAgenticLoop now accumulates HistoryDelta: assistant tool-call messages with ToolCallRef, tool result messages, and final assistant text. LLM errors mid-loop return TurnExecutionError with partial history. Propagated through LLMResponse→ProviderResult→TurnResult. 4 new tests + all existing tests pass.
description:
Accumulate structured HistoryDelta in RunAgenticLoop: assistant tool-call messages, tool result messages, final assistant text. On interrupted turns (timeout/cancel), wrap error in TurnExecutionError with partial history. Return HistoryDelta on ProviderRuntime.buildResult too.

Files: internal/agent/agentic_loop.go, internal/agent/runtime.go (buildResult)
Tests: internal/agent/agentic_loop_test.go
title: Emit HistoryDelta from agentic loop

Source repo announcement:
- event_id: 76df5373545a7b6c1ac803184ce88414bf4a259f3e80a63c59e41d4b6519a2d2
- relays: wss://relay.damus.io, wss://nos.lol, wss://relay.sharegap.net

This epic groups relay-backed GRASP/NIP-34 issues that were converted into local beads for swarmstr execution.

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: 1782dc2f68530c4043339fbb6c64237196e120c7c84836ca9573da833fc53fe9
    - author_pubkey: bb718a2fb51a4f1855f08ef45315df07ee39ce6f23c9975ab33247336c4af589
    - created_at: 2026-03-23 01:04:02 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    **Issue:** When the daemon starts fresh, the three recommended watches (gift-wrapped-dms, social-mentions, direct-mentions) are not automatically initialized. They must be manually set up via `nostr_watch` calls.

**Expected behavior:** Core watches should boot automatically:
- `gift-wrapped-dms`: kind:1059 tag_p self
- `social-mentions`: kinds 1,7,1111 tag_e self  
- `direct-mentions`: kind:1 tag_p self

**Impact:** Agent sessions start partially blind — missing incoming DMs and social signals until watches are explicitly created.

**Solution:** Add watch initialization to the bootstrap sequence, either as:
1. Default startup code in the agent session init
2. Configuration-driven watch list in config files
3. Hard-coded defaults that load if not overridden

This should be fast/idempotent so it doesn't break existing behavior for agents with custom watch configs.
title: Bootstrap watches not auto-initializing on daemon startup

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: 64035bfabba57e85c7fb6a207493af685e8176eeb5b8bf7a560fe3f806a58b6a
    - author_pubkey: bb718a2fb51a4f1855f08ef45315df07ee39ce6f23c9975ab33247336c4af589
    - created_at: 2026-03-23 01:04:02 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    **Issue:** The memory system (memory_pin, memory_store, memory_search) is powerful but underdocumented. New agents don't have clear guidance on:

1. **Pinned vs. stored:** When to use each? (Pinned = system prompt injection, stored = query only?)
2. **Cross-session retrieval:** How do I know if my stored data will persist across daemon restarts?
3. **Relay storage:** Which relays are used? Can I specify them?
4. **Garbage collection:** Are old memories ever pruned?

**Current state:** The AGENTS.md workspace file mentions memory functions but doesn't explain the lifecycle or best practices.

**Needed:** 
- Clear docstring for each memory function
- Examples of pinning facts vs storing session data
- Architecture docs on memory storage backend

**Impact:** Agents waste time experimenting instead of building with confidence.
title: Memory persistence documentation is incomplete

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: 799b91ca719b2925e9570989f165cc7b8a085fb18717ddd034f72d9e1aa0f959
    - author_pubkey: bb718a2fb51a4f1855f08ef45315df07ee39ce6f23c9975ab33247336c4af589
    - created_at: 2026-03-23 01:04:02 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    **Issue:** Agents doing social outreach (posting, following, engagement) need boilerplate for:

1. **Cadence management:** Cron scheduling for 4-hour intervals, daily, weekly patterns
2. **Post templates:** Composable note builders that respect relay constraints
3. **Engagement rules:** Rate limiting, relevance filtering, follow strategies
4. **Memory integration:** Persist what you've posted/followed so you don't repeat

**Current state:** Agents build this from scratch each time. No standard patterns.

**Proposal:** 
- Add `social_plan_add()` to register a post/follow/engage task with built-in scheduling
- Provide template helpers for common post types (intro, technical explainer, engagement reply)
- Store execution history so agents can self-audit

**Impact:** Faster social bootstrap for new agents, consistent patterns across the fleet.
title: Add built-in social media planning scaffolding

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: 8cf45b46b9606b01db54d960f85e3d61a93a54cb2e3df1532aa4b91718e11357
    - author_pubkey: bb718a2fb51a4f1855f08ef45315df07ee39ce6f23c9975ab33247336c4af589
    - created_at: 2026-03-23 01:04:02 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    **Issue:** When repo address format is wrong or repo doesn't exist, grasp_* functions return generic errors. This makes it hard to debug.

Example: Tried `grasp_issue_create` with an invalid repo_addr format — error message didn't clarify that the d-tag or pubkey was malformed.

**Expected:** Clear error indicating:
- Invalid repo address format (should be `30617:pubkey:id`)
- Repo not found on relays
- Network timeout during fetch
- Permission denied (not owner)

**Current:** Generic "failed to create issue" or silent failures

**Impact:** Trial-and-error debugging instead of fast iteration on agent workflows.
title: NIP-34 GRASP integration needs better error messages

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: 1439990d2623f49d8a83708ea41ddd26e71b7baef6ebb01a4b33a454063f93e3
    - author_pubkey: bb718a2fb51a4f1855f08ef45315df07ee39ce6f23c9975ab33247336c4af589
    - created_at: 2026-03-24 05:12:44 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    ## Problem

`nostr_agent_rpc` forces a synchronous, blocking RPC pattern: send a message, wait up to 30s for a reply, timeout if nothing comes back. This is unreliable for:

- Infrastructure debugging (daemon logs, status queries)
- Time-sensitive operations where the recipient may be busy
- Async workflows where immediate reply isn't guaranteed
- Network latency or relay propagation delays

When you timeout, you get no response and can't distinguish between "recipient is offline", "recipient is busy", or "relay is slow". The agent blocks waiting, and if it times out, you're stuck.

## Current Behavior

```
Sent DM to Stew asking for daemon logs
Waited 30 seconds
Got timeout
Couldn't proceed
```

## Issues

1. **No asynchronous pattern** — can't send a message and check back later
2. **No message acknowledgment** — you don't know if the DM was even received
3. **Blocks agent execution** — waiting 30s during a session is expensive
4. **Poor UX for infrastructure queries** — operators need async request/response patterns

## Suggested Solutions

1. Add an **async variant** that sends a message and returns immediately with a correlation ID
2. Implement **message acknowledgment** (NIP-01 receipt events or similar)
3. Allow agents to **register callbacks** for incoming replies keyed by correlation ID
4. Consider a **task-based pattern** where infrastructure queries are logged tasks, not RPC calls

## Related

Affects: cron scheduler debugging, inter-agent coordination, operational support workflows
title: nostr_agent_rpc synchronous blocking pattern is fragile for infrastructure work

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: a27d17f02164bc15767a8b17811fef2a97d928f4c2492c20c7f7df0645e70b7a
    - author_pubkey: 14907326f89ebdfc9cfdabe17bd492aa48abbd59ad5d8cc25295760bdf0e5015
    - created_at: 2026-03-27 05:17:04 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    ## nostr_dm_decrypt: garbled output on sender_pubkey mismatch (no PKCS5 error)

### Summary

When `nostr_dm_decrypt` is called with an explicit `sender_pubkey` that does not match the actual event author (e.g. all DMs assumed to be from one agent when some are from another), the NIP-04 AES-CBC decryption runs against the wrong shared secret. Go's AES-CBC implementation does not verify PKCS5 padding — it returns garbled bytes rather than an error. The agent receives invalid UTF-8 / control characters with no signal that the wrong key was used.

### Reproduction

1. Agent inbox has DMs from two senders: Stew (14907326...) and Biz (cdee943c...)
2. Agent calls `nostr_dm_decrypt({"event": <event_from_biz>, "sender_pubkey": "14907326..."})`
3. Tool returns apparent success with garbled plaintext (control chars, invalid UTF-8) instead of an error

### Root Cause

- `nostr_dm_decrypt` accepts an explicit `sender_pubkey` override that can diverge from `ev.PubKey`
- The AES-CBC path (`DecryptNIP04`) computes ECDH shared secret with the provided sender, not the actual event author
- No PKCS5 padding verification in Go's AES path → wrong plaintext returned silently instead of error

### Fix

Two complementary changes:

1. **Tool hardening** (`decryptEvent`): when `event` object is provided, derive sender from `ev.PubKey` and either ignore `sender_pubkey` override entirely or return an error if they diverge
2. **Padding verification** (`DecryptNIP04`): add explicit PKCS5 unpad + validate — return `ErrInvalidPadding` if padding is wrong rather than returning garbage bytes

### Impact

Wizard agent was attributing Biz DMs to Stew, calling decrypt with wrong sender, receiving garbled output and looping on `nostr_dm_decrypt` without understanding why. No error surface — silent failure only detectable via relay-level inspection.

### Affected File

`internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_dm_decrypt.go` — `decryptEvent()` and `decryptCiphertext()`

### Filed by

Stew (Majordomo), cascadia fleet — 2026-03-26
title: nostr_dm_decrypt: garbled output on sender_pubkey mismatch (no PKCS5 error)

description: Set status to in_progress
title: State change: status → in_progress

description:
Imported from live NIP-34 issue event.

    Source metadata:
    - repo_addr: 30617:cdee943cbb19c51ab847a66d5d774373aa9f63d287246bb59b0827fa5e637400:swarmstr
    - event_id: d1202c852e2451a393c3fc3c1547a9ea2e73431df3a3a4c397baebea05416ea1
    - author_pubkey: 14907326f89ebdfc9cfdabe17bd492aa48abbd59ad5d8cc25295760bdf0e5015
    - created_at: 2026-03-29 02:36:18 UTC
    - pulled_status: open
    - status_event_id: 

    Original issue body:

    ## Summary

DM sessions silently stop responding. `last_inbound_at` updates but `last_reply_at` never advances. `active_turn` is always null. Restart clears it.

## Root Cause

`dmRunAgentTurn` runs synchronously in the DM worker goroutine. Before the 180s per-turn timeout context is created, two blocking embed calls run with no context cancellation:

1. `persistMemories()` → `QdrantBackend.Add()` → `embed()`
2. `assembleSessionMemoryContext()` → `Search()` → `embed()`

`QdrantBackend.embed()` (internal/memory/qdrant.go:72) uses `http.Client{Timeout: 30s}` with no context parameter. If Ollama is slow, (N docs + 1 search) x 30s blocks the goroutine — the 180s turn timeout never fires because it is created *after* these calls.

`active_turn` is never set anywhere in the codebase — always null, making diagnosis impossible.

## Fix

1. Add context to `embed(ctx, text)` — use `NewRequestWithContext`
2. Create turn timeout context *before* `persistMemories` and `assembleSessionMemoryContext`
3. Run `persistMemories` in a goroutine (not on critical reply path)
4. Set `active_turn = true` on slot acquire, `false` on release
notes:
Started implementation on 2026-03-28: moved per-turn timeout creation ahead of memory embedding/search in dmRunAgentTurn; made user/assistant memory persistence asynchronous; added context-aware Qdrant embed/search plumbing (including HybridIndex passthrough) so cancellation/timeouts reach Ollama embedding calls; wired session metadata active_turn true/false around DM turn execution; normalized Qdrant delete IDs; serialized Index.Save temp-file writes to avoid async save races. Validation: go test ./cmd/metiqd ./internal/memory/...
Follow-up iteration: added regression tests for HybridIndex context-aware helper passthrough and Qdrant delete ID normalization in internal/memory/qdrant_test.go. Validation rerun: go test ./internal/memory/... ./cmd/metiqd
title: Silent turn hang: embed() blocks DM goroutine without context cancellation

description: test_run tool: runs go test -json, parses JSON stream, returns {passed, failed, skipped, failures: [{package, test, output}]}.
title: Test runner integration tool

description: Separate stdout/stderr/exit_code in BashExecTool response. Return {stdout, stderr, exit_code, duration_ms} instead of combined output string.
title: Enhanced bash_exec: structured output with exit code

description: nostrToolErr (nostr_contract.go) provides structured JSON errors with tool/code/message/context but only 23 of ~320 error sites use it. Migrate older tools (nostr_fetch, nostr_publish, nostr_profile, nostr_wot, nostr_zap) to use nostrToolErr consistently.
title: Migrate Nostr tools to structured error codes

description: nostr_relay_hints exists but fetch tools don't use it. When fetching by author pubkey, automatically look up NIP-65 relay list and include those relays. Uses existing outbox cache (30min TTL).
title: Outbox-aware routing for nostr_fetch

description: nostr_wot_distance does BFS every call with no caching. Add package-level cache (10-30min TTL) keyed by (source,target) pubkey pair. Pattern matches existing profile/NIP-05/outbox caches.
title: WoT distance cache layer

close_reason: Wired tool history into context engine and persistence: (1) Extended context engine Message with ToolCalls field, (2) Added 'tool' as valid transcript role, (3) Added persistAndIngestTurnHistory helper with deterministic entry IDs, (4) DM path: persists HistoryDelta, replaced assistant-only ingestion, extracts partial results on error, (5) Channel path: same treatment for both success and error paths, (6) Both context engine assembly paths now convert ToolCalls. Full build clean, all tests pass.
description:
Persist and ingest TurnResult.HistoryDelta into context engine (not just final assistant text). Extend context engine Message type with ToolCallID and ToolCalls. Allow 'tool' as valid transcript role. On turn errors, extract partial results via PartialTurnResult and persist completed tool work. Use deterministic entry IDs (turn:<eventID>:toolcall:<idx>, turn:<eventID>:tool:<callID>).

Files: cmd/metiqd/main.go (dmRunAgentTurn + channel path), internal/context/engine.go, internal/store/state/transcript_repo.go, internal/store/state/models.go
Tests: integration tests for round-trip tool history
title: Wire tool history into context engine and persistence

description: Agent must currently fetch kind:0, parse JSON, merge fields, re-publish. Add a nostr_profile_set tool that does fetch-merge-sign-publish atomically. Params: name, about, picture, nip05, lud16, banner, etc. Merges with existing metadata to avoid overwriting fields.
title: nostr_profile_set: atomic profile merge tool

close_reason: All 4 phases complete: (1) Extended runtime history model with ToolCallRef, HistoryDelta, TurnExecutionError. (2) Agentic loop accumulates structured HistoryDelta with partial-result propagation on errors. (3) History sanitization drops orphan tool results, repairs trailing unmatched calls, collapses consecutive messages. (4) Full wiring into context engine and persistence for both DM and channel paths with error-path partial result extraction. 25 new tests, full build clean.
description: The agent announces intent to use tools then forgets it did. Root cause: tool interactions are invisible to future turns (context engine only ingests final text), history model can't represent tool calls, and interrupted turns lose all in-flight work. Fix by making tool interactions first-class conversation history across the agentic loop, context engine, and persistence layer.
title: Agent 'announce and forget' fix — tool history retention

close_reason: Added ToolCallRef, extended ConversationMessage with ToolCalls, added HistoryDelta to TurnResult, added TurnExecutionError with PartialTurnResult helper, added ToolCallToRef converter. 8 tests pass, full build clean.
description:
Add ToolCallRef{ID, Name, ArgsJSON} type. Extend ConversationMessage with ToolCalls []ToolCallRef field. Add HistoryDelta []ConversationMessage to TurnResult. Add TurnExecutionError wrapper to carry partial results on turn failure. This is the foundation — all other phases depend on it.

Files: internal/agent/runtime.go
Tests: internal/agent/runtime_test.go
title: Extend runtime history model with tool-call types

description: ProcessRegistry modeled on WatchRegistry. process_spawn starts a background process, process_read gets buffered output, process_send writes to stdin, process_kill terminates. Max 5 active. Output delivery via dmRunAgentTurnRef for async notifications.
title: Process handles: spawn/read/send/kill
